verbatim_text: But we need to fundamentally change our training techniques to get them to seek informative feedback in uncertain scenarios. We may also need to retrain or train these models in ways such that they identify what are some right sources of information that's available at test time in order to update themselves, maybe update their weights, retrain themselves on that piece of information. And this can also be to learn good representations for that particular task. Now, my second trust involves rethinking sort of pre-training. I mean, so far for algorithm learning, we have relied on pre-training to give us the right priors, but we can only go so far. And if we need to solve new problems, we need new priors to our new skills. And some of that involves things like generating reasonable hypothesis on research problems. And we need to rethink pre-training and how to kind of get that, how to change our data objectives and training pipelines and pre-training in order to build these new priors. And if you really want to scale test time adaptation infinitely, then we also need to sort of tackle the efficiency challenge and the architectural challenge. In many cases, for example, with transformer, there's a certain limit to what you can do with test time adaptation. The memory blows up pretty quickly if you produce more tokens. So we might need to augment some of these training architectures in the right way. And also there can be other uses of pre-training data that go beyond next token prediction. For example, if you want to train value functions that can predict some reasonable notions of future and some long horizon planning problems, we can do that if you try to repurpose the pre-training data in the right way.
